mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0 percent, it signals a labor market inflection point that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations, driving immediate positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances indicate synchronized demand expansion across durable goods and services, which amplifies corporate profit growth and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects peak capacity utilization that precedes margin compression and supply bottlenecks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases correlate with positive 20-day equity returns in more than 50% of late-cycle observations, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues, which translates into higher earnings per share and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a rolling 3-year directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized rate, it triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 65% of the time following CPIAUCSL breaches the 2.5 percent threshold, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market tightening that supports wage-driven consumption and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts negative 20-day returns in over 55% of active windows, or the interaction effect disappears when isolated from FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery phases, a divergence where real GDP growth exceeds industrial production growth signals demand outpacing supply constraints, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The demand-supply divergence signal yields negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during recovery periods, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC drops below 0.02.
